talk_metadata.talk_title: Chemical Trapping of Inactive GRB2 Dimer Unveils a Novel Cancer Vulnerability by Coupling Replication Stress to Antitumor Immunity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't care they're promiscuous. We just want to teach the model how to find binders versus non-binders
Speaker admitted: we have not yet shown how the inhibitor affects focal adhesion kinase directly

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects on focal adhesion kinase not fully characterized

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Selectivity relies solely on dimer interface; many SH2/SH3 proteins may share similar pockets
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad enrollment without biomarker stratification of GRB2 dependency

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that GRB2 knockout cells are sensitive to PARP inhibition, then concluded that the GRB2 inhibitor alone is sufficient for cytotoxicity without addressing PARP synergy

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy in immunocompetent models

crispro_opportunity.description: GRB2 inhibitor induces cGAS‑STING activation via replication stress; CrisPRO could model this innate immune activation to predict combinatorial partners
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: inhibitor increases gamma H2AX and activates cGAS‑STING pathway
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's pathway simulation to identify synergistic immune checkpoint or STING agonist combos

crispro_opportunity.description: No biomarker used to select tumors dependent on GRB2 dimerization
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted specificity concerns due to many SH2/SH3 proteins
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply CrisPRO's predictive modeling to discover expression signatures of GRB2 dependency

cited_competitors.name: KRAS G12C inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as example of SH2/SH3 targeting challenges

unresolved_questions: Does chronic GRB2 inhibition lead to adaptive resistance via alternative signaling pathways?
What is the impact of GRB2 inhibition on normal tissue homeostasis in vivo?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AI‑guided discovery and targeting of Plexin B2 in circulating tumor cell cluster‑driven metastasis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we have 80% true positive rate in predicting antibody performance, highlighting its strong power for antibody prediction
Speaker admitted: we only tested the best AI‑predicted antibody so far

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential expression of plexin B2 in normal monocytes and neurons may cause off‑target toxicity not yet evaluated
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: We observed significant reduction in clusters, but did not report any non‑responders

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies heavily on AlphaFold 3 and proprietary docking scores; competitors could replicate with open‑source models
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Single‑target approach; loss of plexin B2 expression could render therapy ineffective

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed strong in vitro blockade of CTC clusters by anti‑plexin B2, then concluded that targeting plexin B2 alone will be sufficient without addressing potential redundancy with other adhesion molecules

key_data_gaps: No post‑treatment patient samples to assess target loss
Toxicity assessment in normal tissues not presented

crispro_opportunity.description: If plexin B2 antibodies fail due to target loss, CrisPRO can repurpose the AI pipeline to design antibodies against the up‑regulated DL3 target
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker identified DL3 up‑regulation in plexin B2‑low neuroendocrine clusters
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage existing antibody language model to rapidly generate DL3‑specific candidates

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of prospective biomarker to select patients with high plexin B2‑cluster activity
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted plexin B2 enrichment in CTC clusters but no clinical assay described
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Develop CrisPRO‑derived predictive signature integrating CTC‑cluster transcriptomics

cited_competitors.name: Enfortumab vedotin (Nectin‑4 ADC)
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as a successful ADC for bladder cancer

unresolved_questions: Does chronic blockade of plexin B2 affect normal immune cell trafficking?
Can tumors switch to alternative adhesion pathways after plexin B2 inhibition?

talk_metadata.talk_title: SWALL‑1 controls epigenetic methylation, mitochondrial metabolism, and antitumor immunity in IDH mutant gliomas
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we have not tested other IDH mutations beyond R132H
Speaker admitted: we do not know why SWELL‑1 expression is higher in IDH mutant tumors

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Compensatory ion channels (e.g., other LRRC8 subunits) may maintain D‑2HG export
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: We noticed a 25% increase upon AD knockdown, indicating channel composition flexibility

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No patient stratification by IDH1 vs IDH2 mutation type

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Channel is ubiquitously expressed; selectivity may be limited
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad inhibition may affect normal tissues due to VRAC's role in cell volume regulation

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that SWELL‑1 knockout reduces D‑2HG release and improves immune activation, yet concluded that targeting SWELL‑1 will only benefit IDH1‑mutant gliomas without addressing IDH2 context

key_data_gaps: Lack of in vivo pharmacologic inhibitor data
No human tumor biomarker validation of SWELL‑1 expression

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify patients with high SWELL‑1/LRRC8C expression to enrich for responders
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted ~1‑fold increase of SWELL‑1 expression in IDH mutant tumors from TCGA
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to integrate TCGA expression and metabolomics to generate a predictive score

crispro_opportunity.description: Model compensatory up‑regulation of alternative LRRC8 subunits that may bypass SWELL‑1 inhibition
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker observed AD knockdown increased D‑2HG release via AC channel formation
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate channel subunit composition dynamics to design combination strategies

cited_competitors.name: PARP inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Synergy with GRB2 inhibitor discussed earlier

unresolved_questions: Will pharmacologic SWELL‑1 blockade recapitulate genetic knockout effects?
How does SWELL‑1 inhibition affect normal brain cells and blood‑brain barrier integrity?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Beckman Research Institute of the City of Hope
talk_metadata.talk_title: eIF4A1 inhibition to target translation of serine biosynthesis in AML
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: Zoledronate showed no impact on normal hematopoietic cells
Speaker admitted: we have not performed detailed systemic toxicity studies in mice

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects on bone metabolism not evaluated in AML models
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker minimized toxicity by only monitoring mouse weight

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Zoledronate is a known bone‑targeting bisphosphonate; its chemistry may limit tumor penetration

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that >90% of eIF4A1 is free of eIF4F complex, yet concluded that targeting eIF4A1 will not affect global cap‑dependent translation

key_data_gaps: Absence of pharmacodynamic biomarkers in patients

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine eIF4A1 inhibition with MCL1 inhibitors to exploit synthetic lethality
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker identified MCL1 as top sensitizer in CRISPR screen
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can prioritize dosing schedules for synergistic combos

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify AML subsets with high free eIF4A1/YB‑1 complex activity
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed YB‑1 interaction drives PHGDH mRNA stability
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to develop a transcriptomic signature predicting response to zoledronate

unresolved_questions: Will long‑term eIF4A1 inhibition affect normal protein synthesis in non‑hematopoietic tissues?
Can resistance emerge via up‑regulation of alternative RNA helicases?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Targeting cancer stem cells via anti‑Rho2 antibody to overcome ADC resistance in triple‑negative breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: anti‑Rho2 antibody is anti‑human and works in mouse PDXs
Speaker admitted: the kinase activity of Rho2 is still under investigation

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Unclear whether antibody blocks ligand binding or induces receptor internalization; downstream signaling not fully mapped
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Rho2‑negative cells did not form tumors, but no data on potential escape via Rho1 or other pathways

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Rho2 is an orphan receptor; lack of structural data may limit antibody optimization
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Single‑antigen approach; tumors may switch to Rho1 or other stemness pathways

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that anti‑Rho2 reduces stemness markers, yet concluded that targeting Rho2 alone will eradicate therapy‑resistant cells without addressing potential compensatory pathways

key_data_gaps: No clinical safety data for anti‑Rho2 antibody
Mechanistic proof of receptor blockade missing

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine anti‑Rho2 with APR‑246 (p53 reactivator) to exploit redox vulnerability
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed anti‑Rho2 lowers NRF2 targets and sensitizes cells to APR‑246
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model redox network to predict optimal dosing ratios

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop assay to quantify surface Rho2 on patient tumors for enrollment
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker used CITE‑seq to identify Rho2‑positive cells
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage CrisPRO to translate CITE‑seq signatures into a clinical IHC panel

cited_competitors.name: Enfortumab vedotin
cited_competitors.context: Discussed as ADC with resistance due to target loss

unresolved_questions: Does anti‑Rho2 affect normal embryonic‑like cells in adult tissues?
Can tumors up‑regulate Rho1 to bypass Rho2 blockade?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Deletion of Peroxiredoxin 3 Impairs Mitochondrial Bioenergetics and Tumor Growth in Mesothelioma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: thiostrepton is insoluble and challenging to formulate, but we succeeded in a local formulation
Speaker admitted: we have limited patient numbers in Phase 1, making biomarker validation difficult

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential systemic toxicity due to off‑target covalent modification of other cysteine proteins
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Patients with high SLC7A11 expression were non‑responders
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker minimized systemic exposure by intrapleural delivery

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No pre‑selection for PRDX3 high‑expressing tumors
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial monitoring of PRDX3 cross‑linking beyond pleural effusion samples

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Covalent mechanism may affect other peroxiredoxins; scaffold is chemically unstable
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Local delivery restricts applicability to pleural disease only

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that PRDX3 knockout abolishes tumor growth in mice, yet concluded that systemic thiostrepton may be viable despite its poor pharmacokinetics

crispro_opportunity.description: Use SLC7A11 expression to select patients likely to respond to thiostrepton
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker identified SLC7A11 up‑regulation in non‑responding explants
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate RNA‑seq and proteomics to build a predictive response model

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop next‑generation PRDX3 inhibitors with improved solubility and selectivity using AI‑driven scaffold optimization
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned ongoing SAR to reduce off‑target effects
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply CrisPRO's generative chemistry module to design covalent warheads specific for PRDX3 active site cysteines

unresolved_questions: Can systemic PRDX3 inhibition be achieved without off‑target toxicity?
What is the role of PRDX3 in normal mesothelial cells and potential side effects of chronic inhibition?
